macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Upcoming US CPI Data and Key Economic Indicators Highlight Economic Calendar
items.summary: Today's economic calendar features critical US releases including CPI at 12:30 PM (forecast 330.21, prior 332.3), Initial Jobless Claims at 02:30 PM (prior 189K), ADP Employment Change at 12:30 PM (forecast 109K), and Challenger Job Cuts at 12:00 PM (60.62K announced). Other highlights include EA PPI YoY at 09:00 AM (2.1% actual vs 1.6% forecast) and MX Interest Rate Decision at 07:00 PM (expected to hold at 6.75%).

items.title: Arcutis Biotherapeutics Q1 2026 Earnings Miss EPS Expectations
items.summary: Arcutis Biotherapeutics reported Q1 2026 earnings that missed EPS expectations, causing the stock to drop 11.78% in after-hours trading amid investor concerns.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Reaches $81,033 with $1.6T Market Cap
items.summary: Bitcoin's live price stands at $81,033.12 USD, boasting a market capitalization of $1.6 trillion and 24-hour trading volume of $31.5 billion, signaling strong cryptocurrency market activity.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Hires Four More OpenAI Researchers
items.summary: Meta has recruited four OpenAI researchers—Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren—in a continued effort to strengthen its AI capabilities and talent acquisition from competitors.

items.title: Leadership Shakeup Deepens at xAI as Two Co-Founders Exit
items.summary: Two co-founders of Elon Musk's xAI startup, Tony Wu and Jimmy Ba, have resigned within 48 hours, marking the departure of roughly half the core founding team since the company's establishment in 2023 amid competition and regulatory challenges.

items.title: AI Scores High as Math Learning Tool in New Survey
items.summary: A new survey reveals that 68 percent of students use AI tools for math assignments and exams, with nearly 70 percent of top-performing math students incorporating AI into their study habits.

items.title: Anthropic Intensifies Partnership Spree With $1.8 Billion Akamai Deal
items.summary: Anthropic has announced a major partnership deal worth $1.8 billion with Akamai, expanding its enterprise AI capabilities and market reach.

items.title: NASA and IBM's Prithvi AI Model Reaches Orbit in First-Ever Deployment
items.summary: NASA and IBM have successfully deployed their Prithvi AI model in space, marking the first-ever orbital deployment of this collaborative AI system.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: June PlayStation State of Play Reveals Final Fantasy Tactics Remaster, Silent Hill f Release Date, and More
items.summary: The June 2025 PlayStation State of Play showcased major announcements including a remaster of Final Fantasy Tactics titled The Ivalice Chronicles, a release date for Silent Hill f, and trailers for 007 First Light featuring a young James Bond.

items.title: Xbox Leaders Outline New Priorities with Flexible Pricing and Brand Revitalization
items.summary: Microsoft confirms a shift back to the Xbox brand with a 'back-to-basics' approach, promising flexible pricing and focusing on console hardware amid declining PS5 sales.

items.title: MercurySteam Announces Layoffs Ahead of New Title Release
items.summary: Metroid Dread developer MercurySteam has laid off staff, with their newest title set for Steam release later this week.

items.title: Katsuhiro Harada Launches New Studio VS Studio Under SNK as CEO
items.summary: Former Tekken director Katsuhiro Harada has joined SNK to lead VS Studio six months after leaving Bandai Namco.

items.title: Nintendo Announces $500 Switch 2 Bundle with First-Party Game
items.summary: Nintendo revealed a $500 Switch 2 bundle including a first-party game, amid reports of price hikes due to chip shortages.

items.title: Sega Cancels 'Super Game' Plan, Shifts to Classic Reboots and Movies
items.summary: Sega has abandoned its ambitious 'Super Game' initiative to focus on reboots of classic franchises and film adaptations.

items.title: World Video Game Hall of Fame 2026 Inducts Angry Birds, Silent Hill, Dragon Quest
items.summary: The class of 2026 includes Angry Birds, Silent Hill, Dragon Quest, and others in the World Video Game Hall of Fame.

items.title: Valve Sets May 8 Reservations for Second Wave of Steam Controllers
items.summary: Valve opens pre-orders on May 8 for additional Steam Controllers after initial launch issues and bot scalping.

items.title: Xbox and Discord Partner for Game Pass Starter Edition via Nitro
items.summary: Xbox partners with Discord to offer a Game Pass Starter edition through Discord Nitro subscriptions.

items.title: Double Fine Workers Form Union with CWA
items.summary: Employees at Xbox studio Double Fine, known for Psychonauts, are unionizing with the Communications Workers of America.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
